rationale: The "fail bait" hook is a proven casual-puzzle UA pattern and the audience (casual women 25-55) overlaps reasonably with Marble Sort's target, but the execution is built around a Bus Jam-style passenger/vehicle puzzle — the visual vocabulary (saturated greens/blues, vehicles, passenger pickup) directly conflicts with Marble Sort's pastel lavender 3D marble-and-conveyor DNA. Mechanic translation is weak: a "wrong move = crash" framing doesn't map cleanly onto a physics-tap-and-drop sorter where failure is more about mis-timed releases or mixed colors. Only worth testing if reshot natively with marbles overflowing/spilling into the wrong box under a "Can you solve it?" overlay — do not whitelist the original creative.
